title: An Overview of Mass Spectrometry-Based Methods for Functional Proteomics.
abstract: The mechanism underlying many biological phenotypes remains unknown despite the increasing availability of whole genome and transcriptome sequencing. Direct measurement of changes in protein expression is an attractive alternative and has the potential to reveal novel processes. Mass spectrometry has become the standard method for proteomics, allowing both the confident identification and quantification of thousands of proteins from biological samples. In this review, mass spectrometry-based proteomic methods and their applications are described.
